segments.text: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had stayed two days in Ziklag,
on the third day, behold, [^1] a man came out of the camp from Saul, with his clothes torn and earth on his head. When he came to David, he fell to the earth and showed respect.

footnotes.text: + {   "type": "speech",   "text": "Behold" } , from  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "הִנֵּה" }, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

segments.text: David said to him, {
Where do you come from?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He said to him,
I have escaped out of the camp of Israel.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David said to him, {
How did it go? Please tell me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He answered,
The people have fled from the battle, and many of the people also have fallen and are dead. Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David said to the young man who told him, {
How do you know that Saul and Jonathan his son are dead?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The young man who told him said, {
As I happened by chance on Mount Gilboa, behold, Saul was leaning on his spear; and behold, the chariots and the horsemen followed close behind him.
segments.speaker: The young man who

segments.text: When he looked behind him, he saw me and called to me. I answered, ‘Here I am.’
segments.speaker: The young man who

segments.text: He said to me, ‘Who are you?’ I answered him, ‘I am an Amalekite.’
segments.speaker: The young man who

segments.text: He said to me, ‘Please st and beside me, and kill me, for anguish has taken hold of me because my life lingers in me.’
segments.speaker: The young man who

segments.text: So I stood beside him and killed him, because I was sure that he could not live after he had fallen. I took the crown that was on his head and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them here to my lord.
segments.speaker: The young man who

segments.text: Then David took hold on his clothes and tore them; and all the men who were with him did likewise.
They mourned, wept, and fasted until evening for Saul and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of Yahweh, [^2] and for the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.

footnotes.text: + {   "type": "speech",   "text": "Yahweh" }  is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "LORD" } (all caps) in other translations.

segments.text: David said to the young man who told him, {
Where are you from?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He answered,
I am the son of a foreigner, an Amalekite.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David said to him, {
Why were you not afraid to stretch out your hand to destroy Yahweh’s anointed?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David called one of the young men and said, {
Go near, and cut him down!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He struck him so that he died.
David said to him, {
Your blood be on your head, for your mouth has testified against you, saying, ‘I have slain Yahweh’s anointed.’
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: David lamented with this lamentation over Saul and over Jonathan his son
(and he commanded them to teach the children of Judah the song of the bow; behold, it is written in the book of Jashar):
Your glory, Israel, was slain on your high places! How the mighty have fallen!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Don’t tell it in Gath. Don’t publish it in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.
segments.speaker: David
David

segments.text:
From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, 
Jonathan’s bow didn’t turn back. 
Saul’s sword didn’t return empty.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives. In their death, they were not divided. They were swifter than eagles. They were stronger than lions.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text:
You daughters of Israel, weep over Saul, who clothed you delicately in scarlet, 
who put ornaments of gold on your clothing.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: How the mighty have fallen in the middle of the battle! Jonathan was slain on your high places.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text:
I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan. You have been very pleasant to me. 
Your love to me was wonderful, 
surpassing the love of women.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war have perished!
segments.speaker: David
